name: node-debug
title: node-debug
description:
small debugging utility for Node.js
node-debug provides a small debugging utility for Node.js modules.
.
With this module you simply invoke the exported function to generate
your debug function, passing it a name which will determine if a
noop function is returned, or a decorated console.error, so all of the
console format string goodies you're used to work fine.
A unique color is selected per-function for visibility.
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use node-debug because small debugging utility for Node.js.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use node-debug as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair node-debug with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `node-debug --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
